minimal_context: The student is participating in a PE lesson and is instructed to join a team basketball game with their peers.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time to start the team basketball game. Please join your assigned teams on the court.

STUDENT: I'm not playing this. It's a waste of time.

TUTOR: You need to try your best and join the game.

STUDENT: (Steps back from the court) I said I'm not doing it. The teams aren't even fair.

TUTOR: Just go out there and participate with your peers.

STUDENT: (Shoves a peer who walks by) Watch where you're going! I'm out of here, I'm not doing this stupid game!

options.A: Considering the variance and inconsistencies in individual student experiences as a defining feature of their difficulties over time.
options.B: Using the student's levels of engagement as a quick screening assessment during the physical education activities over time.
options.C: Acting persistently and remaining patient and restrained, ensuring the student receives neither an irritated response nor a public reprimand.
options.D: Implementing social skills group interventions to address peer interactions during the physical education activities over time.
